question: primary_problem
answer: Reduce manual PR review workload - Developers spend too much time manually reviewing PRs, want to automate this process

question: core_value_proposition
answer: Use Claude models through AWS Bedrock to automatically review PRs with intelligent, contextual feedback

question: primary_scope
answer: MVP: Core review automation only - Focus on automating the review process using Claude/Bedrock, fetching PR data, claiming mechanism, and reviews as GitHub PR comments

question: what_should_this_project_not_do_initial_mvp_scope
answer: No external integrations - Keep everything self-contained using GitHub API and local processing only

question: primary_users
answer: Development team members - Developers, senior engineers, tech leads who review PRs during their daily work

Question: primary_goal_for_users
answer: Get faster feedback on PRs - catch bugs, security issues, performance problems before merge

Question: pain_points_ranking
answer: Slow feedback loops
Inconsistent review quality
Review bottleneck
context switching overhead

question: success_metrics
answer: Time saved metrics - Track time saved on PR reviews, faster merge cycles, reduced bottleneck

question: success_target
answer: 2-3x more PRs reviewed per day - Increased throughput

question: technical_constraints
answer: All of the above - Must use AWS Bedrock for Claude, must integrate with existing GitHub infrastructure, must be runnable on developer machines

question: timeline
answer: 2-4 weeks - Quick MVP to validate the concept

question: dependencies
answer: GitHub CLI and AWS Bedrock - Use gh CLI for GitHub operations and AWS Bedrock for Claude API access

question: assumptions
answer: Teams use GitHub for PRs and developers have AWS credentials - Standard GitHub workflow is in place with configured Bedrock access

question: risks
answer: Coordination issues - Multiple machines reviewing same PR (race conditions)

question: risk_mitigation
answer: GitHub labels as locks - Use PR labels to claim/lock PRs before review

question: teams_notification_priority
answer: Post-MVP feature - Not needed initially, add after validation

question: pr_type_detection
answer: File path analysis - Changes in apps/react-webapp or lib/core-ui-system = frontend, all other paths = backend, mixed if both

question: skill_selection
answer: Predefined skill mapping - Frontend PR uses frontend skills, Backend PR uses backend skills, Mixed PR uses both

question: configuration_management
answer: Use existing system credentials - AWS Bedrock uses pre-existing environment variables, GitHub CLI uses existing gh auth, minimal additional configuration needed

question: repository_specification
answer: Command-line argument - Pass repo as argument (e.g., ./review-rock validkeys/lumen). Target repository: https://github.com/validkeys/lumen

question: polling_frequency
answer: Every 5 minutes - Balance between responsiveness and API rate limits

question: error_handling
answer: Retry with exponential backoff - Retry failed operations with increasing delays

question: mvp_features
answer: All of the above - Poll for PRs, claim with label, classify type, run review, post comment, plus monitoring/logging and configuration
